name: Beli Mawr
description: Beli Mawr, whose name translates to "Beli the Great," is a significant figure in Welsh mythology, often regarded as an ancestral god-king. His origins are shrouded in mystery, with some accounts suggesting a connection to the Gaulish god Belenos, while others portray him as a native Welsh deity. Beli Mawr is frequently depicted as a powerful ruler and progenitor of many prominent figures in Welsh legend, including kings and heroes. He is considered the ancestor of many Welsh royal lines and a central figure in the founding myths of the Welsh people. Though not directly associated with specific powers or domains in surviving texts, his influence is felt through his descendants and his symbolic role as a unifying ancestor. He represents the lineage, heritage, and sovereignty of the Welsh people. Beli Mawr's legacy persists in genealogical records and literary traditions, solidifying his position as a foundational figure in Welsh cultural identity. His role in the Mabinogion and other medieval Welsh texts, though sometimes indirect, reinforces his importance as a symbol of Welsh kingship and national identity.
subtitle: Great Ancestor and King of the Britons

keyMyths.title: Beli Mawr's Ancestry
keyMyths.description: Beli Mawr is presented as the ancestor of many significant figures and royal lines in Welsh mythology. This lineage solidifies his position as a foundational figure.

keyMyths.title: Connection to the Mabinogion
keyMyths.description: Though not always directly present, Beli Mawr's descendants and the consequences of their actions appear throughout the Mabinogion. His influence is felt through the stories of his lineage.

keyMyths.title: Beli and Anna
keyMyths.description: Beli Mawr is often linked with the figure of Anna, considered to be a goddess or important figure, with their union producing prominent offspring. This union highlights the importance of lineage and divine connection.

sources.author: John Edward Lloyd
